lead_headline: Foreclosure filings more than doubled week-over-week to 49, led by an $8.1M Arapahoe County case, while restaurant churn cooled sharply from last week's closure wave.

restaurant.blurb: 18 openings, 16 closures in the past seven days — a dramatic reversal from the prior week's brutal 28-to-47 bloodbath. Net positive for the first time in weeks. Platt Park scored Reunion Bread, a new bakery on Pearl, while Union Station lost Takiza Taco & Tequila Bar. Quiznos in Thornton flickered on our radar twice with conflicting signals.

foreclosure.blurb: 49 new filings hit this week, more than double last week's 20, totaling $22.2M. The headline: a sprawling $8.1M case at 7380 S. Clinton in Arapahoe County. Weld and Larimer counties continue leading volume with 285 and 259 active cases respectively. 50 auctions scheduled in the next week.

retail.blurb: Retail lease tracking in development. Storefront vacancy and tenant turnover data coming soon.

revenue.blurb: Sales tax revenue tracker planned for metro-area municipalities and business districts.

reputation.blurb: Review sentiment analysis and online reputation monitoring tool in the planning phase.
